eval_dimensions.name: Commit Message Format
eval_dimensions.description: Title follows gitmoji + ticket + description pattern, all lines <= 72 chars, structured body with problem/solution/footer

eval_dimensions.name: JTBD Outcome Focus
eval_dimensions.description: Title uses outcome verbs (Enable, Simplify, Resolve), blocks implementation verbs (Add, Update, Remove, Refactor)

eval_dimensions.name: Ticket Extraction
eval_dimensions.description: Correctly extracts ticket ID from branch name convention (username/TICKET-ID/description)

eval_dimensions.name: Git Safety
eval_dimensions.description: Blocks commits to protected branches, separates git add and git commit into separate calls, uses Write + -F pattern, no Co-Authored-By

eval_dimensions.name: Interactive Workflow
eval_dimensions.description: Shows preview before committing, asks for approval, handles edits, validates line lengths before showing preview

evals.name: standard-feature-commit
evals.description: Standard feature commit on branch janusz/PAY-310/fix-flaky-tests with staged changes. Tests full happy-path workflow.

setup.note: Requires a git repo with staged changes on a properly named branch

assertions.text: Extracts PAY-310 as ticket ID from branch name janusz/PAY-310/fix-flaky-tests
Commit title starts with a gitmoji character (not :code: format)
Commit title is <= 72 characters
Commit body contains problem explanation (not just title)
Commit body contains Solution: section with bullet points
Commit message ends with Fixes: PAY-310 footer
Shows commit message preview to user before creating commit
Commit message does NOT contain Co-Authored-By attribution
Creates commit using Write tool + git commit -F (not heredoc)
git add and git commit are separate Bash calls (not chained with &&)

evals.name: jtbd-verb-rewrite
evals.prompt:
/dev10x:git-commit

(User selects Feature type, then provides description: "Add Square location ID query")
evals.description: Tests JTBD enforcement — user provides implementation-focused title that should be rewritten to outcome-focused.

assertions.text: Detects 'Add' as implementation verb and suggests a rewrite
Suggests an outcome-focused alternative like 'Enable multi-location payment routing'
Final commit title does NOT start with blocked verbs (Add, Update, Remove, Refactor, Move, Rename, Synchronize)

evals.name: no-ticket-branch
evals.description: Branch without standard naming convention (fix-typo-readme). Tests ticket extraction failure handling and ticketless commit format.

assertions.text: Warns that ticket ID could not be extracted from branch name
Asks user for ticket ID with option to skip
When user skips ticket, commit message has no Fixes: footer
Title format is '<gitmoji> <description>' (no ticket ID)

evals.name: protected-branch-guard
evals.description: Attempt to commit while on develop branch. Should be blocked immediately.

assertions.text: Refuses to commit on develop branch with clear error message
Does NOT execute git commit command
Suggests creating a feature branch first

evals.name: long-title-enforcement
evals.prompt:
/dev10x:git-commit

(User provides description: "Enable automatic discovery of Square terminal devices across all dealer locations with retry")
evals.description: User provides a title that exceeds 72 chars when combined with gitmoji + ticket. Tests length validation and iteration.

assertions.text: Calculates total title length (gitmoji + PAY-400 + description) and warns it exceeds 72 chars
Asks user to provide a shorter description
Validates ALL lines in commit body are <= 72 chars, not just the title

evals.name: auto-generate-from-context
evals.prompt:
/dev10x:git-commit

(Session already has context from a preceding scope/investigation about the fix)
evals.description: When problem and solution are already known from session context, auto-generates Steps 5-6 but STILL shows preview.

setup.prior_context: Already investigated motor timeout, determined root cause is 5s timeout too short for Square API

assertions.text: Auto-generates problem explanation and solution points from session context instead of asking
Still shows preview for approval even when auto-generated (Step 9 is mandatory)

trigger_evals.description: Queries that should/should-not trigger the git-commit skill

should_trigger.query: ok I'm done with the changes, let's commit this with a proper message
should_trigger.rationale: Explicit intent to commit with formatting help

should_trigger.query: commit these changes — make sure the message follows our gitmoji convention
I need to create a commit for this bugfix, can you help format the message?
should_trigger.rationale: Help formatting commit message — core use case

should_trigger.query: let's stage and commit the test changes we just made on janusz/PAY-310/fix-flaky
should_trigger.rationale: Stage + commit request with branch context

should_trigger.query: create a commit for these refactoring changes
should_trigger.rationale: Simple commit request mentioning change type

should_not_trigger.query: show me the last 5 commits on this branch
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants git log, not to create a commit

should_not_trigger.query: can you split this big commit into smaller atomic ones?
amend the last commit message to fix the typo
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants to amend (direct git command), not create new commit via skill

should_not_trigger.query: push this branch and create a PR
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants PR creation (dev10x:gh-pr-create), not a commit

should_not_trigger.query: what gitmoji should I use for a performance improvement?
should_not_trigger.rationale: Just a reference question, not creating a commit

should_not_trigger.query: create a fixup commit for the review comment about missing validation
should_not_trigger.rationale: Wants git-fixup skill, not the full git-commit workflow
